English: X75 MotionOS is a hands-free desktop control app that lets you control your Windows computer using webcam-based hand tracking and optional voice commands. Move your finger to control the cursor, pinch to click, hold to drag, scroll with gestures, and use supported voice commands for common desktop actions. The beta is designed for accessibility, productivity, experimentation, and touch-free interaction. Camera frames are processed locally on your device for hand tracking. Voice commands are optional and can be disabled from settings.

Initial public beta release with hand-tracking cursor control, click gestures, scrolling, drag/select support, custom gestures, user profiles, optional voice commands, in-app manual, and installer support.
Control your mouse cursor using webcam-based hand tracking
Pinch to click and double pinch to double click
Hold gestures to drag or select items
Scroll pages using natural two-finger hand movement
Use desktop gestures for task view and desktop switching
Optional voice commands for common desktop actions
Custom gesture mappings for advanced users
Profiles for shared computers and different users
Built-in manual with gesture and voice command guidance
Hands-free mouse control with webcam hand tracking and optional voice commands.
X75 MotionOS is proprietary software. See the included license terms for permitted beta use, restrictions, third-party notices, and warranty disclaimer.

Working webcam required for hand tracking
Good lighting for reliable hand detection
Internet connection may be needed if external speech recognition is enabled
Well-lit workspace with clear hand visibility
